title: azalea glauca var. hispida
intro: Azalea glauca var. hispida, also known as the hairy leaved azalea, is a deciduous shrub native to Japan and the mountainous regions of East Asia, characterized by its small white to pink flowers and hairy leaves. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its various medicinal properties, including antiviral, antibacterial, and anti-inflammatory effects, which are attributed to its high content of saponins and other bioactive compounds. The health benefits of Azalea glauca var. hispida include its ability to boost the immune system, reduce fever, and alleviate symptoms of respiratory tract infections, such as the common cold and influenza. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures, and can be combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
